mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar F-Type R engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned AJ133 5.0-litre supercharged V8 engine typically costs £6,500–£10,000 supply only. Used engines are available from £4,500–£7,500. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500 depending on your location and workshop. Most Jaguar engine replacements range from £3,000 to £9,000. However, dealer quotes for a Jaguar V8 engine replacement can be around £15,000–£30,000. Reconditioned engines are available in the £8,000–£10,000 range.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar F-Type R engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ133 5.0-litre supercharged V8 is generally considered a great engine, but it has known weak points. The most significant issues are timing chain tensioner wear (particularly on early models with aluminium guide rails), water pump failures, and supercharger coupler wear. With proper maintenance (oil changes every 8,000 miles, not the 16,000-mile interval), the AJ133 can be a durable and rewarding engine.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar F-Type R?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most frequently reported issues are timing chain tensioner wear (rattling on cold start), water pump failures (coolant loss, belt squeal), supercharger coupler wear (rattling/clunking from the top of the engine), plastic coolant pipe cracking, and injector issues. The F-Type R also has known subframe corrosion issues on some models.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar F-Type R engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept £50,000 F-Type R, an £8,000 recon is 16% — worth it. Against a tired £20,000 example, the same recon approaches our 60% Jaguar threshold — get a scrap valuation first. Well-maintained F-Type Rs are appreciating — a proper restoration is often justified.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar F-Type R engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The F-Type R carries the engine code AJ133. It's a 5.0-litre supercharged V8 petrol engine. Early R models produce 550 PS; later R models and the SVR produce 575 PS. The badge on the tailgate is the easiest tell — 'R' or 'SVR'.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain tensioners, or does it need full engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative timing chain and tensioner replacement costs £1,500–£4,000. Early 5.0L models used aluminium guide rails that were weaker than the steel tensioner, which would dig into the guide and wear it down over time. This issue was revised by JLR in 2015 with a steel insert. If ignored and the chain snaps, the engine is an interference design — pistons and valves collide, resulting in catastrophic engine damage. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Regular oil changes (every 8,000 miles) are the single most effective preventative measure.

replacementCosts.h2: Jaguar F-Type R Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the AJ133 5.0-litre supercharged V8 engine. Most Jaguar engine replacements range from £3,000 to £9,000. However, dealer quotes for a Jaguar V8 engine replacement can be around £15,000–£30,000. Reconditioned engines are available in the £8,000–£10,000 range. Main dealer new engines can exceed £25,000.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an F-Type R engine typically takes 16–22 hours at £80–£120/hr (independent) or £140–£200/hr (London/SE dealer). The supercharged V8's complexity and tight engine bay make this a specialist job.

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear — AJ133 V8
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start lasting a few seconds, metallic ticking from the engine, engine management light, cam/crank correlation fault codes, eventual catastrophic failure if ignored.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles (can occur earlier on hard-driven examples)
problems.repairCost: £1,500–£4,000 (timing chain kit + tensioner replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.replacementCost: £6,500–£14,000 (if chain skips and pistons strike valves)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: Timing chain tensioner wear is the most significant issue on the AJ133 V8. Early 5.0L models used aluminium guide rails that were weaker than the steel tensioner, which would dig into the guide and wear it down over time. This issue was revised by JLR in 2015 with a steel insert, but continued failures have been reported beyond this date. If you hear a rattle on cold start, get the timing chain system inspected immediately. Regular oil changes are essential — the specified service interval of 16,000 miles is too long; change oil at half that mileage.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: Water Pump & Coolant System Failures
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, belt squeal, low coolant warning light, overheating, white smoke from exhaust.
problems.repairCost: £500–£1,200 (water pump replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.recommendation: Water pump failures are a common issue on the 5.0-litre supercharged V8. The engine can be particularly susceptible to cooling system issues when driven hard, such as on a circuit. The water pump also drives the supercharger belt, making replacement a more involved job. Many specialists recommend proactively replacing the water pump around 60,000 miles, especially on the supercharged version.

failureLink.label: Read the full coolant leak guide

problems.title: Supercharger Coupler Wear
problems.symptoms: Rattling or clunking noise from the top of the engine, especially at idle or when the engine stops, unusual supercharger whine.
problems.repairCost: £600–£1,500 (supercharger coupler replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.recommendation: The supercharger coupler (also known as the torsion isolator) is a known weak point on both the V6 and V8 F-Types. A loud "clonk" from the top of the engine when it stops is a classic symptom. There is a Jaguar Technical Bulletin (JTB 00356NAS2) for this issue. The original coupler had a spring which could break; the upgrade is to a solid coupler. If you hear rattling or clunking, have the supercharger coupler inspected.

failureLink.label: Read the full supercharger failure guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: F-Type R — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches the fault before catastrophic failure
Well-understood fix; solid coupler upgrade available
Replace with aluminium pipes for a permanent fix
Typically requires full engine replacement or complete rebuild

buyingChecks.title: Buying This F-Type R? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2015 build (revised timing chain guides with steel inserts)
* Documented timing chain service history or proof of inspection
* Water pump replaced within the last 40,000 miles
* Full service history with evidence of regular oil changes — ideally every 8,000 miles, not the 16,000-mile interval
* No cold-start rattle, coolant loss, or supercharger noise
avoidIf: * Cold-start rattle with no timing chain history
* Coolant loss with no documented water pump or cooling system work
* Rattling from the supercharger area (coupler wear)
* Poor service history — the AJ133 is a high-performance engine that demands meticulous maintenance
* Engine warning light with timing or cooling system codes and no diagnosis

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The F-Type R is a genuine Jaguar icon — a 5.0-litre supercharged V8 in a lightweight sports car chassis. The AJ133 is a great engine with a proven track record across the JLR range. However, it has known weak points: timing chain tensioners, water pumps, supercharger couplers, and plastic cooling system components. These are well-understood and fixable issues, but they demand attention and budget. Buy on documented history, not on faith. A well-maintained F-Type R is a £40,000–£70,000 future classic; a neglected one is a £10,000 engine replacement waiting to happen.

cta.label: → Compare F-Type R engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar F-Type R (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or ticking (timing chain tensioner warning)
* Service History — including the AJ133 V8 era (2014–present) and confirmed oil-change intervals — ideally every 8,000 miles, not the 16,000-mile interval
* Coolant Check — Any history of coolant loss or water pump replacement? (common on the 5.0 SC)
* Supercharger Noise — Any rattling, clunking, or unusual whine? (coupler wear warning)
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup can indicate coolant ingress
* Timing Chain Check — Post-2015 build preferred (revised timing chain guides)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved timing, cooling, or supercharger issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
